channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Agents arrest man reportedly seen on viral video entering Colorado apartment with gang members
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man killed by stray weight at Colorado track and field meet remembered as dedicated husband, dad
item.description: Relatives of a spectator killed by an errant weight thrown at a youth track and field meet in Colorado say he was a dedicated father with a great sense of humor.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Evacuated residents wait hours for a chance to return to burned LA neighborhoods
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Residents anxious to see what had become of their properties after the Palisades Fire ignited on Jan. 7 waited in their cars for hours Tuesday to return to their neighborhoods after officials lifted the last remaining evacuation orders.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan Democratic Sen. Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening a key Senate seat in 2026
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Sen. Gary Peters, who led the Democrats’ Senate campaign efforts the past two election cycles, has announced he will not seek a third term in 2026, creating a highly contested battleground seat expected to be coveted by both major political parties.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firings, freezes and layoffs: A look at Trump's moves against federal employees and programs
item.description: President Donald Trump has implemented sweeping changes in the first days of his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Donald Trump and Project 2025 previewed the federal grant freeze
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A White House order to freeze federal grants reflects a theory of presidential power that Donald Trump clearly endorsed during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Senate confirms Sean Duffy to lead Department of Transportation
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) to be the next Transportation secretary, putting him in place to lead a sprawling agency that oversees air travel, highways, pipelines and railroads.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Killer mom Susan Smith is now 'complete nightmare' after parole was denied: report
item.description: South Carolina killer mom Susan Smith, who drowned her two sons, is reportedly acting like a "complete nightmare" behind bars, months after she was denied parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House press briefing discusses federal assistance, deportation
item.description: Karoline Leavitt addressed the media from the White House's briefing room for the first time on Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Shooting at Indiana grocery store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: When police “engaged” the shooting suspect outside the store, two officers were wounded and the suspect was fatally shot, according to the Elkhart Police Department.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No one injured after Border Patrol, cartel shootout in Texas
item.description: (NewsNation) — U.S. Border Patrol was involved in a shootout with cartel members in the Rio Grande Valley, the Texas Department of Public Safety says. The tense moments unfolded Monday in Fronton, Texas. At around 1:40 p.m, shots were fired from the Mexican side of the border at agents.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple software update will activate AI by default, but there is a way to turn it off
item.description: iPhone users could have a somewhat surprising new feature on their devices after their next software update.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma proposal requires parents to provide immigration status when enrolling students
item.description: Parents enrolling children in Oklahoma public schools will be required to provide proof of their child’s U.S. citizenship or legal immigration status under a proposal rule approved by the State Board of Education.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kash Patel’s podcast persona: Staunch Trump defender and fierce critic of the FBI he could soon lead
item.description: Kash Patel positioned himself as a steadfast Donald Trump loyalist well before the president picked him to run the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Ali Swenson And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: California utility reports fault on power line miles away from origin of deadly Eaton fire
item.description: GLENDALE, Calif. (AP) — Southern California Edison on Monday reported a fault on a power line connected miles away from ones located near the origin of the Eaton Fire, the deadly blaze that ignited outside of Los Angeles on Jan. 7 and killed at least 17 people.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Paris Hilton reunites foster dog with family affected by Eaton Fire
item.description: Now that's sweet. Paris Hilton took part in a heartwarming reunion between an Altadena family and their beloved dog, Zuzu. "Letting go is never easy, but moments like this remind me why fostering is so important," she wrote on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter pardoned by Trump killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KTLA 5

item.title: PETA suggests replacing Punxsutawney Phil with a ‘weather reveal' cake
item.description: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals, or PETA, suggested that Groundhog Day be celebrated with a “weather cake” instead of pulling Punxsutawney Phil from his burrow to determine if winter will be prolonged or if spring will come early.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl footballs are ready to go within hours of the matchup being set
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action. The factory in the rural village of Ada, Ohio, makes the game balls used by every NFL team along with many of the nation’s top college programs and high schools.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks offering free refills to all customers, bringing back condiment bar
item.description: Starbucks is implementing new and old policies at its cafes in the United States and Canada to improve the customer experience. Beginning on Monday, customers who order a “for here” beverage can also get free refills of hot or iced coffee or hot or iced tea during their visit.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — After a half-century of public silence, a freelance photographer from Vietnam has asserted he took one of the most renowned and impactful photos of the 20th century — the image of a naked girl fleeing a napalm attack in South Vietnam that has long been credited to a staff phot
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Ohio State celebrates national championship run amid change and farewells
item.description: Fans braved 30-degree temperatures to welcome home a team that beat five top-10 rivals on its way to the title, including Oregon in the Rose Bowl, Texas in the Cotton Bowl and Notre Dame in the College Football Playoff championship game.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: For four years, the Environmental Protection Agency made environmental justice one of its biggest priorities, working to improve health conditions in heavily-polluted communities often made up largely of Black, Latino and low-income Americans. Now that short-lived era is over.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Air Force has removed training courses with videos of its storied Tuskegee Airmen and the Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — the female World War II pilots who were vital in ferrying warplanes for the military — to comply with the Trump administration's crackdown on dive
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Nuns have snowball fight during winter storm
item.description: Several nuns duked it out last week, having a snowball fight during a winter storm that swept across the Deep South.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
